name: pybind11 (C++ Python Bindings)

performance.notes: Near-zero overhead for binding calls; compiled C++ performance

agent_readiness.notes: Header-only C++ library for creating Python bindings of C++ code. Expose C++ classes, functions, STL containers to Python with minimal boilerplate. NumPy array integration. Automatic type conversions. Exception propagation. Virtual function overriding. Thread safety controls. Used by major Python ML libraries (PyTorch, OpenCV, etc.) internally. Alternative to Cython/CFFI for C++ specifically. nanobind is the successor with better performance.

gotchas: Long compile times for complex bindings
ABI compatibility issues between Python versions
nanobind is faster and preferred for new projects
best_when: Wrapping existing C++ libraries for Python use
Writing Python extensions that call C++ for performance
Exposing C++ numerical code to Python/NumPy pipelines
Any project requiring mature, stable C++/Python interop
avoid_when: Pure Python performance (use Numba or PyPy)
New C++ extension projects (nanobind is faster compilation)
When Cython suffices for simpler C extension needs
summary: pybind11 is a header-only C++ library for creating Python bindings of C++ code. Minimal boilerplate — expose C++ classes and functions to Python with near-zero overhead. NumPy integration. Used internally by PyTorch, OpenCV, and many major Python ML libraries. The standard choice for C++/Python interop. For new projects, consider nanobind (pybind11's faster successor).
